title: WF-015 Audit integrity verification — tamper-evidence on the append-only audit log
business_reason: Fifth (final) chain under the workflow-at-a-time rule (WF-015). createAuditEvent already writes a hash chain (prev_hash + event_hash) and audit_events is append-only, but nothing VERIFIED it. Add the verifier: recompute each event's hash and detect any tampering/forgery, opening a critical security incident — the audit-hash-verify + finding->incident steps of WF-015.

design.single_source: Refactored createAuditEvent to export auditCore() + auditEventHash(); the verifier REUSES them so verification can never drift from the writer.
design.verifier: server/audit/verifyAuditChain.ts: PURE verifyAuditEvent(row) recomputes sha256(prev_hash + canonical core) and compares to the stored event_hash (per-event integrity — independent of the in-process chain, so it holds across restarts). verifyAuditChain(rows) -> {ok, checked, tampered, tampered_ids}. Unit-tested.
design.route: GET /api/staff/security-audit/verify-chain: verifies the most-recent events (newest-first so a freshly-tampered row is in window); on failure opens (or reuses) a CRITICAL security_incident referencing the offending event ids; audited.
design.immutability: audit_events is append-only — UPDATE and DELETE are blocked at the DB (verified in the e2e); a forgery can only be APPENDED, and the verifier catches its bad hash.
design.ui: /staff/security-audit/integrity page (verify button + result + incident), nav via STAFF_CUSTOM_NAV.

param_14.question: How do we know the audit log hasn't been tampered with?
param_14.answer: Every audit event commits to sha256(prev_hash + its canonical content). The verifier recomputes that from the stored row; any mismatch means the row was tampered or forged, and a critical security incident is opened referencing it. The audit log is also append-only (UPDATE/DELETE blocked at the DB), so existing rows cannot be altered or removed — a forgery can only be appended, and it is detected.
param_14.answer_source: createAuditEvent hash chain (prev_hash/event_hash), audit_events append-only trigger, WF-015
param_14.security_implications: tamper-evidence for the entire audit trail (per-event hash recompute, single-source with the writer); append-only immutability enforced at the DB; tampering -> a critical incident, audited; the verifier itself never mutates the log.
param_14.approval_implications: none (read + incident open); incident remediation follows the security_incidents lifecycle.
param_14.downstream_dependencies: full-log pagination (currently the most-recent 1000); permission_drift_findings scan -> incident; scheduled integrity sweep worker.
param_14.assumptions: the hash logic is stable (auditCore/auditEventHash are the single source for write + verify).
